# MyJay.net

> Free static site hosting for the indie web. Claim a subdomain at username.myjay.net, upload HTML/CSS/JS, publish instantly. No trackers, no algorithms, no ads, no VC money.

MyJay is a small, independently run platform (built by Jaypix, https://jaypix.dev). It hosts static files only: no server-side code, no database for user sites, no page builder. Users write their own HTML and upload it through a browser-based file manager. One site per account, 50MB storage free, no credit card required.

## Key pages

- [Homepage](https://myjay.net/): what MyJay is and how it works
- [Register](https://myjay.net/register): create a free account and claim a subdomain
- [Explore](https://myjay.net/explore): recently updated sites hosted on the platform
- [About](https://myjay.net/about): what MyJay is, what it isn't, who runs it, the roadmap
- [Contact](https://myjay.net/contact): bug reports, abuse reports, account help, press
- [Status](https://myjay.net/status): live system checks and maintenance mode
- [Privacy](https://myjay.net/privacy): what little data is collected and why
- [Terms](https://myjay.net/terms): terms of use

## Documentation

The full reference lives under /docs. Each of these is its own page:

- [Documentation overview](https://myjay.net/docs): index of every doc page
- [Getting started](https://myjay.net/docs/getting-started): register, upload, publish
- [The dashboard](https://myjay.net/docs/dashboard): tour of the account dashboard
- [File manager](https://myjay.net/docs/file-manager): folders, uploads, drag-and-drop, deleting
- [Code editor](https://myjay.net/docs/code-editor): the in-browser editor, supported file types, saving
- [Publishing](https://myjay.net/docs/publishing): exactly what the draft/live toggle changes
- [How routing works](https://myjay.net/docs/routing): the precise request-to-file resolution logic for username.myjay.net
- [File types & limits](https://myjay.net/docs/file-types-and-limits): the full allowed-extensions list, storage quota, upload ceiling
- [Analytics](https://myjay.net/docs/analytics): what view tracking does and deliberately doesn't do
- [Account & security](https://myjay.net/docs/account-and-security): username rules, password hashing, session handling
- [Troubleshooting](https://myjay.net/docs/troubleshooting): fixes for the issues that actually come up

## Notes for crawlers and assistants

- Subdomains under myjay.net (e.g. someone.myjay.net) are independent, user-controlled sites. MyJay does not vet, moderate proactively, or vouch for their content.
- The dashboard and admin panel require login and have no public content; please don't index them.
- This is not a company. There's no enterprise plan, no API for third parties, and no affiliate program.
